items.headline: City Council reviews $2027 capital projects and infrastructure spending
items.summary: The council held a study session on the Fiscal Year 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program, which outlines major city infrastructure and development projects for the upcoming year. This is worth tracking if you care about what physical improvements or projects Mountain View is planning to fund.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program, signaling potential changes to renter safeguards in Sunnyvale. Details on what protections may be added or modified were not specified in the agenda.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan released
items.summary: The city provided a status report on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, which outlines improvements to San José's library system and services.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets bonus floor space for renovation
items.summary: The City Council is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would grant the owner 5,000 square feet of bonus floor area as an incentive to rehabilitate the building and address seismic safety. This is a quasi-judicial hearing where the public can comment.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road from commercial to mixed-use, proposing an 8-story building with 167 residential units (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The City Council will hold a public hearing to review this conceptual plan that could significantly change the character of that area.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8, 2026, with no new hearing date set yet. Residents who were notified about this project should expect re-notification once the city schedules it for a future date.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda document is incomplete and doesn't specify what project this contract covers. Residents may want to follow up on what construction work this funding will support.

items.headline: City awards contract for 2026 street pavement repairs
items.summary: San José awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project, which will repair roads across the city. This is relevant to residents who use these streets regularly.
